title: Hey Judy | Thoughts & Stories from Judy Faulkner
excerpts:
Section Title: Hey Judy > Thoughts and Stories > Story > Another Tribute to Neil
Content:
February 23, 2026
Neil Pappalardo, who founded MEDITECH in 1969, passed away in January at the age of 83. He created a great company.
Neil helped Epic get a start. He and others at MEDITECH shared advice with me; for example, how to assign offices, what to do about titles, forms to fill out such as for vacation—and everything they shared was very helpful. They care for their customers, they focus on technology, and they never went public, so they avoid the tyranny of the quarter. Epic holds MEDITECH in high regard.
Years ago, when they were helping us get started, Neil invited me to his home for dinner. I realized it was unusual when his kids asked why a piece of folded cloth was next to each plate. I felt honored to be there.
See previous posts
Subscribe
About Us
Subscribe
About Us
Privacy Policy Copyright © Epic Systems Corporation 2026.
Epic CEO and founder Judy Faulkner shares her thoughts and stories from Epic's history, based on her personal philosophies and experiences over the years.

title: Trust, Focus, and the Long View: Epic on the Acquired Podcast | Epic
excerpts:
Section Title: Trust, Focus, and the Long View: Epic on the Acquired Podcast
Content:
In [this episode of *Acquired* ,](https://www.acquired.fm/episodes/epic-systems-mychart) Ben Gilbert and David Rosenthal dive into Epic’s story. They explore how a long-term mindset, customer focus, and development-driven culture have helped Epic gain the enduring trust of many of the world’s leading healthcare organizations.
Ben and David also discuss how Epic’s software has contributed to improved clinical outcomes—including the prevention of millions of potential adverse drug events and surgical errors each year—and how [MyChart](https://www.mychart.org/) helps patients stay connected to their care.
While acknowledging the complexity and challenges of healthcare today, Ben and David tell the story of Epic as a company that has succeeded by maintaining a focused, long-term commitment to doing right by its customers.
Listen [here,](https://www.acquired.fm/episodes/epic-systems-mychart) or wherever yo

title: Ep. 15: The Record-Keeper, with Epic’s Judy Faulkner | Milken Institute
excerpts:
Section Title: Ep. 15: The Record-Keeper, with Epic’s Judy Faulkner
Content:
The story is familiar, even mythic: brilliant young student builds out a new technology in her garage and changes the world. But Judy Faulkner never made it to Silicon Valley. The medical software company she founded in a Madison basement four decades ago remains in Wisconsin—while Epic’s importance to the world of health care continues to grow.
More than 250 million patient medical records are on the Epic platform, and that data could yield valuable clues in the search for solutions to the pandemic. It’s all part of the employee-owned company’s mission to support the heroes on the front lines.

title: The Academy Table Podcast: A Conversation with Judy Faulkner | Epic
excerpts:
Section Title: The Academy Table Podcast: A Conversation with Judy Faulkner
Content:
In this episode, Judy Faulkner, founder and CEO of Epic, brings her philosophy of, “you have to laugh to learn” to The Table. Judy shares from her years of leadership experience about her thoughts on the future of healthcare, future orientation for decision-making, and making a difference with the culture inside your organization.

title: Pro Q&A: Judy Faulkner - POLITICO
excerpts:
Section Title: Pro Q&A: Judy Faulkner
Content:
Judy Faulkner shared her thoughts in response to the overall emphasis on opening EHR data for IT developers. | AP Photo
By [Arthur Allen](https://www.politico.com/staff/arthur-allen)
[](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://politi.co/2SIX3Yf)
[](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://politi.co/2SIX3Yf&text=Pro Q&A: Judy Faulkner&via=politico)
We interviewed Epic Systems founder and CEO Judy Faulkner the day after CMS and ONC released their rules defining the demands on providers, health plans and EHR vendors for information sharing. Like most of the health IT world, Faulkner hadn’t digested the rules, but she shared thoughts in response to the overall emphasis on opening EHR data for IT developers. The interview was lightly edited for space.
**What do you think of the interoperability rules?**

title: Judy Faulkner & Epic Systems: …–Investor Deep Dive – Apple Podcasts
excerpts:
Section Title: Judy Faulkner & Epic Systems: How a Founder Built a Healthcare Software Giant Without VC
Content:
[Investor Deep Dive](https://podcasts.apple.com/am/podcast/investor-deep-dive/id1867299874)
Play
**How did Judy Faulkner build Epic Systems into one of the largest healthcare software companies in the world—without venture capital or Silicon Valley influence?**
In this episode, we break down the extraordinary founder story of **Judy Faulkner** , CEO and founder of **Epic Systems** , the dominant electronic health records (EHR) platform used by leading hospitals worldwide. This podcast explores how Epic grew into a multi-billion-dollar healthcare technology company by rejecting VC funding, prioritizing long-term thinking, and maintaining founder-led control.

title: How Epic's 82-year-old CEO Judy Faulkner built her software factory
excerpts:
Section Title: Bill Gates meets Willy Wonka: How Epic's 82-year-old billionaire CEO, Judy Faulkner, built her so...
Content:
Published Sat, Aug 16 202511:58 AM EDT
[Ashley Capoot](https://www.cnbc.com/ashley-capoot/) [@/in/ashley-capoot/](https://linkedin.com//in/ashley-capoot/)
WATCH LIVE
Key Points
Judy Faulkner, who just turned 82, has spent the past 46 years running Epic Systems, turning it into one of the largest private technology companies in the U.S.
While Faulkner said she still has more to accomplish as CEO, sources told CNBC that the likely choice as her successor is Sumit Rana, who was named president last August.
"Why be owned by people whose interest is primarily return of equity?" Faulkner said in an interview, referring to her aversion to the public market.
Judy Faulkner, founder and chief executive officer of Epic Systems Corp., during the Forbes Healthcare Summit in New York, Dec. 5, 2023.
Michael Nagle | Bloomberg | Getty Images

title: Who We Are: Our Story
excerpts:
Section Title: Who We Are: > Roots & Wings is a family foundation launched in 2020 by Judy and Gordon Faulkner.
Content:
Judy Faulkner is the founder and CEO of [Epic](https://www.epic.com) , a privately-held healthcare software company based in Wisconsin. Her husband, Gordon Faulkner, is a retired pediatrician who provided free care to children in Wisconsin for more than 15 years. Their life’s work – in business and philanthropy – has been to keep people well, help sick people get better, and create a world where all children can reach their full potential.

title: Best Leaders 2025: Judy Faulkner | U.S. News
excerpts:
Section Title: Best Leaders 2025: Judy Faulkner
Content:
Arriving home from work one day, [Judy Faulkner](https://www.epicshare.org/hey-judy) saw her husband, Gordon, was distraught. A pediatrician, he had just learned that a young girl previously under his care in Madison, Wisconsin, had died. Her family had moved to Milwaukee, 75 miles away, and when the girl fell ill, the physicians at a new hospital didn’t know how to treat her because they had no ability to access her medical records. Faulkner’s anguished husband insisted that if the hospital staff had had the girl’s medical history, they’d have known what to do and would have saved her life.
The next day, Judy went back to work, determined to do what she could to prevent such a tragedy from ever happening again.
Judy Faulkner is the founder and CEO of Epic Systems, a software company that maintains patients’ electronic medical records for large health care providers including hospitals, medical centers, rehab facilities, hospices

title: Building Epic: Judy Faulkner on leadership
excerpts:
Section Title: Building Epic: Judy Faulkner on leadership > Tammy Lovell
Content:
**In this episode of *Digital Health Unplugged* , Judy Faulkner, founder and chief executive of Epic Systems, shares her journey from maths puzzles and early programming to building one of the world’s most widely-adopted electronic patient record (EPR) systems.**
Speaking to Tammy Lovell, editor at *Digital Health* , Faulkner reflects on how she started [Epic](https://www.epic.com/) in a basement in 1979 with a handful of programmers, with the company valued at $70,000.
The US software giant has grown into the largest EPR vendor by market share , with its revenue estimated $5.7bn in 2024.
Epic’s revenue is now an estimated $5.7bn in 2024, with the firmcovering more than half of hospital patients in the US.

title: Judy Faulkner's philosophy on interoperability - Becker's Hospital Review | Healthcare News & Analysis
excerpts:
Section Title: Judy Faulkner’s philosophy on interoperability
Content:
Epic founder and CEO Judy Faulkner said her company helped pave the way for interoperability in healthcare by getting health systems to buy into data exchange.
Advertisement
By: Giles Bruce
Monday, August 5th, 2024
Print
Share
 ... 
Section Title: Judy Faulkner’s philosophy on interoperability
Content:
“The CEO did not ask what the software did,” [Ms. Faulkner](https://www.beckershospitalreview.com/ehrs/judy-faulkners-no-1-rule-of-software.html) wrote. “He explained to the group of CEOs [at an Epic meeting] that if he had known what it was for, he would not have signed it! Lucky break, finally, for Interoperability! Congratulations to the CIO and CEO who provided that break, Scott Joslyn and Barry Arbuckle!”
Epic helped launch Carequality, a national interoperability framework for care coordination, and was the first EHR vendor to enroll in information-sharing group TEFCA, according to Ms. Faulkner.

Faulkner** (born August 11, 1943) is an American billionaire businesswoman who is the CEO and founder of Epic Systems , a healthcare software company located in Verona, Wisconsin . <sup>[[ 2 ]]()</sup> Faulkner founded Epic Systems in 1979, with the original name of Human Services Computing. <sup>[[ 3 ]]()</sup> In 2013, *Forbes* called her "the most powerful woman in healthcare", <sup>[[ 4 ]]()</sup> and as of July 2024, estimated her net worth at US$7.8 billion. <sup>[[ 5 ]]()</sup>

title: Amazon.com: TRAILBLAZER : The Extraordinary Journey of Judy Faulkner: The Biography of the Co-founder of Epic Systems; Pioneering the Future of Healthcare Technology ... of America's Richest Self-Made Women) eBook : Grant , Alex: Kindle Store
excerpts:
Section Title: TRAILBLAZER : The Extraordinary Journey of Judy Faulkner: The Biography of the Co-founder of Epic...
Content:
by Alex Grant (Author) Format: Kindle Edition
 ... 
Section Title: TRAILBLAZER : The Extraordinary Journey of Judy Faulkner: The Biography of the Co-founder of Epic...
Content:
Through the pages of this book, discover the audacity and determination that led Judy Faulkner to co-found Epic Systems in a modest basement, armed with a vision and a $70,000 investment. Follow her as she navigates the complexities of building a company, steadfastly refusing venture capital or acquisition by other entities, and maintaining her commitment to privacy and data security. Learn how she defied conventional wisdom by staying privately held, ensuring that Epic Systems remains true to its core values and prioritizes the long-term impact over short-term gains.

title: Epic Systems CEO Judy Faulkner MS’67 named “Best Leader” by U.S. News – Computer Sciences – UW–Madison
excerpts:
Section Title: Epic Systems CEO Judy Faulkner MS’67 named “Best Leader” by U.S. News
Content:
Arriving home from work one day, [Judy Faulkner](https://www.epicshare.org/hey-judy) saw her husband, Gordon, was distraught. A pediatrician, he had just learned that a young girl previously under his care in Madison, Wisconsin, had died. Her family had moved to Milwaukee, 75 miles away, and when the girl fell ill, the physicians at a new hospital didn’t know how to treat her because they had no ability to access her medical records. Faulkner’s anguished husband insisted that if the hospital staff had had the girl’s medical history, they’d have known what to do and would have saved her life.
The next day, Judy went back to work, determined to do what she could to prevent such a tragedy from ever happening again.
Read the full article at: https://go.wisc.edu/faulknerusnews
Posted in [News](https://www.cs.wisc.edu/category/news/)

title: Faulkner, Judith – Computer Sciences – UW–Madison
excerpts:
Section Title: Judith Faulkner
Content:
Judy Faulkner is CEO and founder of Epic, which she began in 1979 in the basement of an apartment house with $70,000 in start-up money and two half-time assistants.  Epic has grown by its bootstraps, without venture capital or going public.
Judy received honorary doctorates from the University of Wisconsin-Madison and from Mount Sinai in New York, an M.S. in computer sciences from the UW, and a BS in mathematics from Dickinson College.  After teaching computer science for several years, she then worked as a software developer, creating one of the first databases organized around the patient.
Judy currently serves on the UW-Madison Computer Sciences Board of Visitors, is a member of the National Academy of Medicine’s Leadership Roundtable and of the Aspen Health Strategy Group.  She has pledged that 99% of her assets will go to philanthropy.

Dec 6, 2024
[### 2024 Forbes Healthcare Summit | The Future Of Care Delivery](https://www.forbes.com/video/9186daea-0e0c-457d-9e1a-78ae252e2411/)
As hospitals and health systems move further into the digital age, healthcare delivery is becoming more personalized, efficient and effective. Moira Forbes, ForbesWomen President & Publisher and Forbes EVP, sits down with Epic Founder & CEO Judy Faulkner and NYU Langone Health and Dean CEO Robert I. Grossman, M.D. -- two visionaries who share their insights on the trends shaping the future of care delivery and discuss strategies to produce better outcomes in a rapidly changing environment which includes shifting away from fee-for-service medicine to value-based care that requires healthcare to be delivered in the right place, at the right time

title: Judith R. Faulkner is an American billionaire businesswoman who is the CEO and founder of Epic Systems, a healthcare software company located in Verona, Wisconsin. Faulkner founded Epic Systems in 1979, with the original name of Human Services Computing.
excerpts:
Section Title: World’s Billionaires List > The Richest People In The World
Content:
The Forbes World’s Billionaires list is a snapshot of wealth using stock prices and exchange rates from March 1, 2026. Some people become richer or poorer within days of publication. We list individuals rather than multigenerational families who share fortunes, though we include wealth belonging to a billionaire’s spouse and children in certain cases, namely if that person is the founder of the fortune. For Russian billionaires, fortunes were calculated using ownership structures from February 2022, prior to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine and before many made transfers of assets to managers, friends and others in an effort to protect their holdings from sanctions. We value a variety of assets, including private companies, real estate, art and more. We don’t pretend to know each billionaire’s private balance sheet (though some provide it). When documentation isn’t supplied or available, we discount fortune
